asset.name: asset

arch.name: arch
arch.title: Architecture this OS is running on

eol.name: eol
eol.title: Asset platform end-of-life status
eol.desc: Examine the End-of-Life metadata for the asset's detected platform: the EoL date itself, a vendor product-page URL, and a documentation URL. Used by audits to flag hosts running platforms whose vendor has stopped issuing security patches.

family.name: family
family.title: List of platform families that this platform belongs to

kind.name: kind
kind.title: High-level platform category (e.g., api, baremetal, virtualmachine, container, container-image, network)

name.name: name
name.title: Human readable name of the asset

platform.name: platform
platform.title: Platform for this asset (redhat, windows, k8s-pod)

platformMetadata.name: platformMetadata
platformMetadata.title: Platform metadata such as distribution and release details

runtime.name: runtime
runtime.title: Specific runtime the asset is operating in (e.g., docker-container, podman-container, aws-ec2-instance)

title.name: title
title.title: Human-readable title of the platform (e.g., "Red Hat 8, Container")

asset.title: Asset identity and platform metadata
asset.desc: Examine the basic identity of the asset under evaluation. Surfaces the human-readable name, the full set of asset identifiers, the platform (e.g., redhat, windows, k8s-pod), the platform `kind` (api, baremetal, virtualmachine, container, container-image, network), the specific `runtime` (docker-container, podman-container, aws-ec2-instance, …), the platform version, build, architecture, and pretty `title`, the platform `family` list, the optional FQDN, the per-platform metadata map, and any user-supplied `labels` or `annotations`.

asset.eol.name: asset.eol
asset.eol.title: Asset platform end-of-life status
asset.eol.desc: Examine the End-of-Life metadata for the asset's detected platform: the EoL date itself, a vendor product-page URL, and a documentation URL. Used by audits to flag hosts running platforms whose vendor has stopped issuing security patches.

cpe.name: cpe

other.name: other
other.title: Miscellaneous CPE attributes not covered by the standard fields

part.name: part
part.title: CPE part designation: a (application), h (hardware), or o (operating system)

cpe.title: Common Platform Enumeration (CPE)
cpe.desc: Examine a parsed CPE identifier (NIST NVD's structured product identifier). Initialize with `cpe(uri: "cpe:2.3:a:vendor:product:1.0:...")` to break the CPE down into its part designation (a / h / o), vendor, product, version, update, edition, language, software edition, target software, target hardware, and miscellaneous fields.

mondoo.name: mondoo

build.name: build
build.title: Build identifier of the client (e.g., production, development, or commit hash)

jobEnvironment.name: jobEnvironment
jobEnvironment.title: Environment context the agent is running in (e.g., scheduled, interactive, CI)

version.name: version
version.title: Version of the client running on the asset

mondoo.title: MQL runtime and execution environment
mondoo.desc: Examine the client running the current MQL evaluation: the client version and build identifier, the architecture string, the connection capabilities reported for the active connection, and the `jobEnvironment` dict describing the context the agent is running in (scheduled, interactive, CI, etc.).

parse.name: parse
parse.title: Common parsers
parse.desc: Namespace for cross-cutting parsing helpers that apply to any connection: `parse.date(value, format)` returns a typed `time` from a string and an optional format, and `parse.duration(value)` converts a duration string into a typed `time` interval.

product.name: product

releaseCycleInformation.name: releaseCycleInformation
releaseCycleInformation.title: End of life information for a product release

product.title: Product end-of-life lookup
product.desc: Examine the End-of-Life metadata for an arbitrary named product. Each instance carries a product name and version and exposes a typed `releaseCycle` resource with the matching release-cycle information from Mondoo's product catalog.

product.releaseCycleInformation.name: product.releaseCycleInformation

name.name: name
name.title: Name identifier of the release cycle

product.releaseCycleInformation.title: End of life information for a product release

regex.name: regex
regex.title: Built-in regular expressions
regex.desc: Examine a library of pre-defined regex patterns for common formats: IPv4 / IPv6 addresses, HTTP/HTTPS URLs, email addresses, MAC addresses, hyphen-delimited UUIDs, emoji code points, semantic version numbers, and credit card numbers.

time.name: time

now.name: now
now.title: The current time on the local system

today.name: today
today.title: The current day starting at midnight

tomorrow.name: tomorrow
tomorrow.title: The next day starting at midnight

time.title: Date and time helpers
time.desc: Examine helpers for time-aware MQL expressions. `now` returns the current local time; `today` and `tomorrow` return midnight on those days; `second`, `minute`, `hour`, and `day` are unit constants used when computing durations.

unicode.name: unicode
unicode.title: Unicode text classification
unicode.desc: Examine a Unicode-classified view of an input string. Initialize with `unicode(input: "...")` and inspect the per-character classification (each character's code point, category, and description) and the per-category histogram showing how many characters fall into each Unicode general category.

category.name: category
category.title: Category is the Unicode general category code (two-letter code)

char.name: char
char.title: Character is the actual Unicode character as a string

codePoint.name: codePoint
codePoint.title: CodePoint is the Unicode code point in U+XXXX format

description.name: description
description.title: Description is the human-readable description of the category

majorCategory.name: majorCategory
majorCategory.title: Major Category is the major Unicode category (single letter)

position.name: position
position.title: Position is the zero-based index of the character in the original string

uuid.name: uuid

urn.name: urn
urn.title: RFC 2141 URN form of the UUID (e.g., urn:uuid:xxxxxxxx-...)

uuid.title: UUID (RFC 4122 / DCE 1.1)
uuid.desc: Examine a parsed UUID. Initialize with `uuid(value: "xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx")` and inspect the canonical string representation, the URN form (`urn:uuid:...`), the version (1, 4, 5, …), and the variant.

exchange.name: exchange
exchange.title: Vulnerability Exchange information (experimental)
exchange.desc: Examine a single vulnerability identifier as published by an upstream feed: the vulnerability `id` (e.g., CVE-2025-12345) and the `source` registry that published it (NVD, GHSA, …).

vulnerability.exchange.name: vulnerability.exchange

source.name: source
source.title: Source/registry that published the vulnerability record (e.g., NVD, GHSA)

vulnerability.exchange.title: Vulnerability Exchange information (experimental)
vulnerability.exchange.desc: Examine a single vulnerability identifier as published by an upstream feed: the vulnerability `id` (e.g., CVE-2025-12345) and the `source` registry that published it (NVD, GHSA, …).
